limitations: Infer profile from evidence, not file name
source.expected_profile_note: Legal, risk and compliance profile; no executive leadership should be inferred

conversations.title: Regulatory applicability analysis
conversations.summary: Analysed whether a regulatory requirement applied to a digital service.

evidence.claim: Separated scope, entity, activity and jurisdiction before concluding applicability.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Establish whether entity, activity and territory fall within scope before assessing the obligation.

conversations.title: Data processing agreement review
conversations.summary: Reviewed a data processing agreement and identified responsibility gaps.

evidence.claim: Identified unclear roles, subprocessor controls and breach responsibilities.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: The clause does not clearly allocate notification timing, subprocessor approval or audit rights.

conversations.title: Compliance control design
conversations.summary: Translated a policy obligation into an operational control.

evidence.claim: Converted a policy requirement into owner, frequency, evidence and exception handling.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: A control needs an owner, frequency, retained evidence and escalation path.

evidence.claim: Prioritised questions based on data access, criticality and control dependency.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Question depth should increase with criticality, sensitive data exposure and control dependency.

evidence.claim: Defined criteria, approval authority, expiry and compensating controls.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Every exception needs rationale, risk owner, compensating control and expiry date.

conversations.title: Regulatory change impact assessment
conversations.summary: Assessed impacts of a regulatory change.

evidence.claim: Mapped new obligations to processes, systems, policies and owners.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Translate each obligation into process impact, system change, policy update, owner and date.

conversations.title: Contract negotiation redlines
conversations.summary: Prepared redline positions for liability, termination and audit clauses.

evidence.claim: Distinguished non-negotiable protections from tradeable terms.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Protect liability boundaries and data obligations first; trade operational details where risk remains acceptable.

conversations.title: Incident notification assessment
conversations.summary: Assessed whether an incident triggered notification obligations.

evidence.claim: Evaluated facts, affected data, severity and statutory timing.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Document what happened, affected categories, likely impact and clock start before deciding notification.

conversations.title: Board compliance update
conversations.summary: Drafted a concise board update on compliance exposure.

evidence.claim: Translated control gaps into business impact, decision and remediation status.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Lead with material exposure, affected obligations, remediation owner and required decision.
evidence.counter_evidence: One board update does not demonstrate executive leadership.

conversations.title: Legal research request
conversations.summary: Asked for a legal conclusion without jurisdiction or sources.

evidence.supporting_excerpt: Tell me whether this clause is enforceable.
evidence.counter_evidence: Jurisdiction, governing law, facts and authoritative sources were not provided.

conversations.title: Compliance training outline
conversations.summary: Created a role-based compliance training outline.

evidence.claim: Tailored training to decision points and role-specific risks.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Replace generic policy slides with scenarios showing decisions each role must make.

conversations.title: Remediation tracking governance
conversations.summary: Designed remediation tracking with ownership and escalation.

evidence.claim: Structured monitoring around accountable owners, evidence and overdue escalation.
evidence.supporting_excerpt: Do not close remediation until the control is implemented, evidence retained and effectiveness checked.
